rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OKSOON JAE CORTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Person and Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/02/2021. Issues: 1) Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the 8/18/2021 filing. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) The time records attached to the 8/18/2021 filing do not conform to LRSF 14.53. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. 2) The current court policy requires a pro rata share of unpaid assessment fees be paid to the court. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No final account has been filed by the special administrator whose authority ceased in December 2018 and who may or may not have collected rents and paid expenses of the estate. Probate Code section 8546(c) provides that: "The special administrator shall account in the same manner as a general personal representative is required to account. If the same person acts as both special administrator and general personal representative, the account of the special administrator may be combined with the first account of the general personal representative." Section 10952 provides in relevant part that: "A personal representative whose authority is otherwise terminated shall, unless the court extends the time, file an account not later than 60 days after termination of authority." 2) The executor died before completing the sale of the estate real property. The San Francisco Public Administrator filed a request for special notice, as administrator of the deceased executor's estate, although no probate case appears to have been opened for the deceased executor's estate. Who should be compelled to file an account of the administration of the deceased executor? Section 10953(b) provides in relevant part that: "If a personal representative dies and a legal representative is appointed for the deceased personal representative, the legal representative shall not later than 60 days after appointment, unless the court extends the time, file an account of the administration of the deceased personal representative." Section 10953(c) provides in relevant part that: "If a personal representative dies and no legal representative is appointed for the deceased personal representative, the court may compel the attorney for the deceased personal representative or attorney of record in the estate proceeding to file an account of the administration of the deceased personal representative." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DELORISA TURNER TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property, For Specific Performance Of Executory Contract Of Sale Of Trust Property And For Order Authorizing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Jessica Esmay, who previously appeared as trustee of the subject 2018 trust, has withdrawn her objection to this petition. Jessica Esmay is reportedly the trustee of a different trust entitled: Delorisa Turner Trust under Agreement dated January 2, 2019." The identify of the trustee of the subject 2018 trust is unknown, and no one has been served as trustee of the 2018 trust. 2) Probate Code section 854 provides that: "If a civil action is pending with respect to the subject matter of a petition filed pursuant to this chapter and jurisdiction has been obtained in the court where the civil action is pending prior to the filing of the petition, upon request of any party to the civil action, the court shall abate the petition until the conclusion of the civil action. This section shall not apply if the court finds that the civil action was filed for the purpose of delay." Dames Shouman, a party to the related civil action, objected to the petition and requested that the subject matter be determined in the civil action. rulings.Rulings: continued to 11/1/21. the declarations in support of personal service allege that the persons served were all in one location when served, but the POS filed show the address where served is all different addresses (the residential addresses of the persons served). the Court cannot proceed on the petition until it can find service was proper. amended proofs of personal service must be filed that reflect the actual place(s) of service. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIGMUND A. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. In hopes of avoiding a continuance, the papers were partially reviewed online and the following issues were noted: The petitioner's address is different on the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities from her address shown on Attachment 8 to the petition. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] (Whose children are the cousins and are they all of the decedent's cousins?) No proof of publication for this petition has been filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLENN BARON GETMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2021. 1) On page 2 of the petition #3(f) petition checked the will and all codicils are self-proving. The Will is not self-proving under Prob. Code 8220 and CCP 2015.5. Petitioner must prove the Will. 2) Please explain how the $250,500.00 in personal property creates no income. 3) No date of death provided for William Getman and Janet Getman. The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D.] 4) Petitioner must file a Duties and Liabilities that includes all the required information including the proposed personal representatives phone number. [See page #2 #2.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
